name: SQLite MCP Server
description: A Model Context Protocol server that provides read and write access to local SQLite databases. Enables LLMs to execute SQL queries, inspect schemas, and manage data through standardized tool calls.
author: modelcontextprotocol

name: GitHub MCP Server
description: Interact with GitHub APIs through MCP. Manage repositories, issues, pull requests, code search, and file operations — giving autonomous agents full-cycle software development capabilities.
author: modelcontextprotocol

name: Filesystem MCP Server
description: Secure, configurable filesystem access for MCP agents. Supports reading, writing, listing, searching, and moving files — with sandboxed directory restrictions for safe autonomous operation.
author: modelcontextprotocol

name: Brave Search MCP
description: Web and local search capabilities via Brave Search API. Enables agents to perform real-time web searches, news lookups, and local business queries with privacy-first, ad-free results.
author: modelcontextprotocol

name: Shodan MCP Server
description: Attack surface reconnaissance via Shodan's internet-wide scanning database. Query exposed services, CVEs, open ports, and organization-wide exposure reports — purpose-built for ASM agents.
author: BurtTheCoder

name: Nmap MCP Server
description: Network discovery and port scanning through MCP. Enables security agents to run configurable Nmap scans, parse results, and feed structured host/service data into automated vulnerability workflows.
author: vorotaai

name: PostgreSQL MCP Server
description: Read-only PostgreSQL access for LLM agents. Inspect schemas, run analytics queries, and retrieve relational data with safe, connection-pooled access to production or staging databases.
author: modelcontextprotocol

name: Docker MCP Server
description: Manage Docker containers, images, volumes, and networks via MCP. Gives DevSecOps agents the ability to spin up containers, run workloads, and manage container lifecycles.
author: QuantGeekDev

name: Semgrep MCP Server
description: SAST (Static Application Security Testing) via Semgrep rules directly in MCP. Agents can scan codebases for security anti-patterns, secrets, and vulnerabilities as part of automated code review pipelines.
author: semgrep

name: Slack MCP Server
description: Send messages, read channels, manage workspaces, and search Slack history. Lets orchestration agents deliver alerts, status updates, and incident notifications into team communication channels.
author: slackapi

name: Puppeteer MCP Server
description: Headless browser automation through MCP. Capture screenshots, fill forms, scrape dynamic pages, and automate web interactions — essential for agents that need to test or monitor web applications.
author: modelcontextprotocol

name: AWS Knowledge Base MCP
description: Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) against Amazon Bedrock Knowledge Bases. Provides agents with semantic search over enterprise document stores, FAQs, and internal wikis stored in AWS.
author: modelcontextprotocol

name: Sequential Thinking MCP
description: Structured step-by-step thinking and planning capabilities. Allows agents to break down complex tasks, plan execution paths, evaluate hypotheses, and dynamically adjust reasoning strategies.
author: modelcontextprotocol

name: Memory Graph MCP
description: Persistent semantic knowledge-graph memory for AI agents. Enables LLMs to construct, read, write, and query fact-relationship networks to maintain long-term memory across chat sessions.
author: modelcontextprotocol

name: Fetch Web Page MCP
description: Fetch web page content and convert it into clean, readable Markdown. Optimized for LLMs to read documentation, APIs, and online articles without HTML or script noise.
author: modelcontextprotocol

name: Notion Workspace MCP
description: Interact with Notion workspaces through MCP. Retrieve page contents, query databases, append blocks, and search workspace pages to give agents access to team wikis and document hubs.
author: makenotion

name: Linear Issues MCP
description: Linear project tracker integration. Search issues, create new tasks, update tickets, and inspect backlogs to help autonomous project management agents orchestrate development cycles.
author: modelcontextprotocol

name: GitLab Integration MCP
description: Interact with GitLab API. Manage repositories, issues, merge requests, groups, and build pipelines — giving autonomous agents full-cycle GitLab development capabilities.
author: modelcontextprotocol

name: Sentry Error Analyzer
description: Interface with Sentry to retrieve application issues, stack traces, and release logs. Enables automated debugging agents to discover and analyze production errors.
author: modelcontextprotocol

name: Google Maps API MCP
description: Provides agents with geo-location, search, mapping, and direction calculations using the Google Maps platform APIs. Ideal for travel and logistics agent routing.
author: modelcontextprotocol
